name: Epic E-4 ECR Registry
description: Build a reusable Terraform ECR module that provisions one private repository per microservice (8 total) with scan-on-push, lifecycle policies, and environment-driven tag mutability (MUTABLE dev / IMMUTABLE prod). Wire the module into the dev and prod environment root modules, provide the list of service names via variables/tfvars, and verify with terraform fmt and terraform validate. Optionally create scripts/ecr-login.sh for Docker authentication to the registry.

goal.answer: Build a reusable Terraform ECR module that provisions one private repository per microservice (8 total) with scan-on-push, lifecycle policies, and environment-driven tag mutability (MUTABLE dev / IMMUTABLE prod). Wire the module into the dev and prod environment root modules, provide the list of service names via variables/tfvars, create scripts/ecr-login.sh for Docker authentication to the registry, and verify with terraform fmt and terraform validate.

criteria.answer:
terraform/modules/ecr/ contains versions.tf, variables.tf, main.tf, outputs.tf with provider constraint ~> 5.0 and required_version >= 1.6.0
ECR module creates one aws_ecr_repository per service under the petclinic-{env}/ namespace for all 8 services
ECR module enables image_scanning_configuration scan-on-push for every repository
ECR module attaches an aws_ecr_lifecycle_policy keeping the last 10 tagged images and expiring untagged images after 7 days
ECR module supports configurable image_tag_mutability (MUTABLE for dev, IMMUTABLE for prod)
ECR module outputs repository_urls and repository_arns maps keyed by service_name
terraform/environments/dev/main.tf calls the ECR module and passes all 8 service_names
terraform/environments/prod/main.tf calls the ECR module and passes all 8 service_names
terraform/environments/dev/terraform.tfvars and prod/terraform.tfvars define service_names with the 8 services
scripts/ecr-login.sh exists, is executable, authenticates Docker to the ECR registry in us-east-2, and accepts an optional --region parameter
terraform fmt -recursive -check terraform/ passes
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/dev
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/prod

constraints.answer: Terraform AWS provider ~> 5.0, Terraform >= 1.6.0, region us-east-2., ECR repos must be per-environment under petclinic-{env}/ namespace., Tag immutability must be MUTABLE for dev and IMMUTABLE for prod., No secrets or credentials committed in code., Do not run terraform apply without explicit approval.

phases.answer: Build ECR module, Wire ECR into environments, Create ECR login helper

assumptions.answer: VPC and EKS modules are already implemented and validated (E-2 and E-3 complete).; State backend bucket/table already bootstrapped (E-1 complete).; terraform init has been run in both environment directories, or can be re-run as needed.; AWS credentials are available in the local environment for terraform plan/validate.; Docker CLI is available for testing the ecr-login.sh script, but no actual push is required.

phases.name: Build ECR module
phases.goal: Create the reusable Terraform ECR module with repository resources, lifecycle policies, scan-on-push, tag mutability, and outputs.
phases.description: Implement terraform/modules/ecr/versions.tf, variables.tf, main.tf, and outputs.tf. The module must accept project, environment, service_names, image_tag_mutability, and tags variables; create aws_ecr_repository resources with scan-on-push; attach aws_ecr_lifecycle_policy resources; and output maps of repository URLs and ARNs.

steps.description: Create terraform/modules/ecr/versions.tf with Terraform >= 1.6.0 and AWS provider ~> 5.0 constraints.

verification.command: grep -q 'required_version = ">= 1.6.0"' terraform/modules/ecr/versions.tf && grep -q 'version = "~> 5.0"' terraform/modules/ecr/versions.tf && echo 'versions OK'

steps.description: Create terraform/modules/ecr/variables.tf declaring project, environment, service_names, image_tag_mutability, scan_on_push, and tags variables.

verification.command: grep -q 'variable "service_names"' terraform/modules/ecr/variables.tf && grep -q 'variable "image_tag_mutability"' terraform/modules/ecr/variables.tf && echo 'variables OK'

steps.description: Create terraform/modules/ecr/main.tf using aws_ecr_repository (for_each over service_names) with image_scanning_configuration and image_tag_mutability, plus aws_ecr_lifecycle_policy resources.

verification.command: grep -q 'aws_ecr_repository' terraform/modules/ecr/main.tf && grep -q 'aws_ecr_lifecycle_policy' terraform/modules/ecr/main.tf && echo 'main.tf OK'

steps.description: Create terraform/modules/ecr/outputs.tf exporting repository_urls and repository_arns maps.

verification.command: grep -q 'repository_urls' terraform/modules/ecr/outputs.tf && grep -q 'repository_arns' terraform/modules/ecr/outputs.tf && echo 'outputs OK'
cd terraform/modules/ecr && terraform fmt -check && terraform init -backend=false && terraform validate

phases.summary: Phase 1 complete: ECR module files (versions.tf, variables.tf, main.tf, outputs.tf) created and validated. terraform fmt -check and terraform validate passed inside terraform/modules/ecr/.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Wire ECR into environments
phases.goal: Call the ECR module from dev and prod root modules, pass the service_names list, declare variables and tfvars, and run project-wide formatting/validation.
phases.description: Update terraform/environments/dev/main.tf and prod/main.tf to call module.ecr. Declare service_names variable in both variables.tf files and set the 8 service names in both terraform.tfvars. Run terraform fmt -recursive -check terraform/ and terraform validate in both environments.

steps.description: Update terraform/environments/dev/main.tf to call module.ecr with service_names from var.service_names.

verification.command: grep -q 'module "ecr"' terraform/environments/dev/main.tf && grep -q 'service_names' terraform/environments/dev/main.tf && echo 'dev ecr module OK'

steps.description: Update terraform/environments/dev/variables.tf to declare service_names variable.
Update terraform/environments/dev/terraform.tfvars with the list of 8 service names.
Update terraform/environments/prod/main.tf to call module.ecr with service_names from var.service_names.

verification.command: grep -q 'module "ecr"' terraform/environments/prod/main.tf && grep -q 'service_names' terraform/environments/prod/main.tf && echo 'prod ecr module OK'

steps.description: Update terraform/environments/prod/variables.tf to declare service_names variable.
Update terraform/environments/prod/terraform.tfvars with the list of 8 service names.
Run project-wide terraform fmt check and validate both environments.

verification.command: terraform fmt -recursive -check terraform/ && cd terraform/environments/dev && terraform validate && cd ../prod && terraform validate

phases.summary: Wired the ECR module into both dev and prod environments: module calls, service_names variables, and tfvars populated for all 8 services. Project-wide terraform fmt and validate passed in both environments. Prod main.tf was corrected to use hardcoded project and no undefined local tags.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Create ECR login helper
phases.goal: Create scripts/ecr-login.sh that authenticates Docker to the ECR registry in us-east-2.
phases.description: Implement a portable shell script that uses aws ecr get-login-password and pipes it to docker login for the account's ECR registry. Accept an optional --region parameter defaulting to us-east-2.

steps.description: Create scripts/ecr-login.sh with AWS account detection, default region us-east-2, optional --region argument, and docker login command.

verification.command: test -x scripts/ecr-login.sh && grep -q 'aws ecr get-login-password' scripts/ecr-login.sh && grep -q 'docker login' scripts/ecr-login.sh && echo 'ecr-login OK'

steps.description: Validate script syntax with shellcheck or bash -n.

phases.summary: Created scripts/ecr-login.sh with AWS account detection, default region us-east-2, optional --region argument, docker login via aws ecr get-login-password, made it executable, and validated syntax with bash -n.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

Wire the module into the dev and prod environment root modules, provide the list of service names via variables/tfvars, create scripts/ecr-login.sh for Docker authentication to the registry, and verify with terraform fmt and terraform validate.
successCriteria: terraform/modules/ecr/ contains versions.tf, variables.tf, main.tf, outputs.tf with provider constraint ~> 5.0 and required_version >= 1.6.0
ECR module creates one aws_ecr_repository per service under the petclinic-{env}/ namespace for all 8 services
ECR module enables image_scanning_configuration scan-on-push for every repository
ECR module attaches an aws_ecr_lifecycle_policy keeping the last 10 tagged images and expiring untagged images after 7 days

constraints: Terraform AWS provider ~> 5.0, Terraform >= 1.6.0, region us-east-2.

grade.rationale: The diff only shows unrelated region-string edits in .claude files, docs, and metadata; none of the required ECR module files, environment main.tf/tfvars, or scripts/ecr-login.sh appear in the visible changed-file list or diff snippets. The phase gate rationales cite only generic terraform fmt/validate without grounding in specific artifact files, and the final summary claims a complete ECR module that the diff does not substantiate, making coverage suspect.
